claim_boundary: These generated records are local, non-normative candidate audit controls. They do not close the handoff tasks, resolve independent review, satisfy an external release gate, certify a consumer or deployment, or authorize publication.
coverage_policy: The exact union of conformance normative requirements, phased release gates, surface/package/non-claim entries, and threat-control requirements must appear once. Every node retains code, test, evidence, command, claim-tier, and status links.

requirements.requirement: ADR-001 (Separate simulation-service and plant-control sessions) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-002 (Separate contract identity and release authorization) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-003 (Authenticate production ingress before interpretation) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-004 (Attach observers with bounded grants and revocation) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-005 (Declare and retire every stream explicitly) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-006 (Use body-issued authority and receiver-local time) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-007 (Journal body-issued command dispositions) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-008 (Separate stable routes from Galadriel extensions) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-009 (Bind semantic security state, rotation, and revocation) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-010 (Specify finite per-plane QoS and overload behavior) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.
ADR-011 (Fix ecosystem dependency direction and plant handover) must receive every required same-digest owner and independent review before normative implementation or candidate rebaseline.

Wire version compatibility is a fail-closed gate.
Every JSON envelope is decoded within the universal resource budget.
Identity role, entity, plane, profile, and security digest fail closed.
Legacy translation is explicit, visible, one-way, and ambiguity rejecting.
Session-scoped messages bind a live session generation.
Actuation and lifecycle mutation require a bounded matching authority lease.
Lifecycle mutations carry idempotency context and terminal receipts.
Stable capabilities and channel requirements are explicit closed choices.
Simulation output never claims a calibrated posterior or reproduction.
TTL, horizon, geofence, speed, HOLD, and ESTOP decisions are deterministic.
Plant negotiation binds a content-addressed plant profile.
Errors carry a registered code; version and session identity fields fail closed.
Every stable message kind has a canonical wire vector.

Prevent or fail closed for payload identity is mistaken for authentication as specified by NCP-THREAT-001.
Prevent or fail closed for authenticated peer exceeds enrolled authority as specified by NCP-THREAT-002.
Prevent or fail closed for stale generation or stream position is replayed as specified by NCP-THREAT-003.
Prevent or fail closed for version text masks contract or algorithm drift as specified by NCP-THREAT-004.
Prevent or fail closed for syntactically valid but contradictory state grants success as specified by NCP-THREAT-005.
Prevent or fail closed for resource exhaustion triggers permissive recovery as specified by NCP-THREAT-006.
Prevent or fail closed for dependency feature unification activates excluded code as specified by NCP-THREAT-007.
Prevent or fail closed for partial 0.8 migration invents missing context as specified by NCP-THREAT-008.
Prevent or fail closed for crash separates mutation outcome from durable evidence as specified by NCP-THREAT-009.
Prevent or fail closed for synthetic output is promoted to scientific evidence as specified by NCP-THREAT-010.
Prevent or fail closed for protocol estop is mistaken for physical safety certification as specified by NCP-THREAT-011.
Prevent or fail closed for production profile downgrades or starts partially configured as specified by NCP-THREAT-012.
Prevent or fail closed for route and payload refer to different live sessions as specified by NCP-THREAT-013.
Prevent or fail closed for ambiguous delivery reuses an action position as specified by NCP-THREAT-014.
Prevent or fail closed for unicode, duplicate keys, or path tricks create cross-language ambiguity as specified by NCP-THREAT-015.
Prevent or fail closed for package namespace or dependency provenance is misattributed as specified by NCP-THREAT-016.
Prevent or fail closed for candidate evidence is relabelled as release authorization as specified by NCP-THREAT-017.
Prevent or fail closed for copied protocol files create a silent consumer fork as specified by NCP-THREAT-018.
Prevent or fail closed for a proposed decision silently enters the normative contract as specified by NCP-THREAT-019.
Prevent or fail closed for simulation and plant session authority domains are confused as specified by NCP-THREAT-020.
Prevent or fail closed for commander handover admits a delayed stale command as specified by NCP-THREAT-021.
Prevent or fail closed for deny-only assessments widen permission through lifecycle as specified by NCP-THREAT-022.
Prevent or fail closed for a read-only or research component enters the plant control path as specified by NCP-THREAT-023.
Prevent or fail closed for simulation provenance is treated as plant authorization or scientific validation as specified by NCP-THREAT-024.
